title: 10 best herbal teas for gerd
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gerd are infusions of plants and spices that can help alleviate the symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). These teas can reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and improve digestion, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of gerd symptoms. Some examples of herbal teas that help with gerd include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger tea can help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with gerd can experience relief from their symptoms, leading to improved quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with GERD because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the digestive system. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux. Additionally, lemon balm's carminative effects help to ease digestion and relieve bloating, further reducing symptoms of GERD. By promoting relaxation and calming the stomach, lemon balm teas provide a natural solution for individuals seeking relief from heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Lemon balm tea has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and neutralizing acid reflux symptoms by counteracting excess gastric acid production.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils present in lemon balm tea help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to GERD symptoms like heartburn and regurgitation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms like discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain compounds that help to soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemon balm leaves from a trusted source or store dried ones in airtight containers.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried lemon balm or one tablespoon of fresh leaves for every eight ounces water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and let it cool down to 195°f to 205°f for steeping.
Steep the lemon balm mixture in hot water for seven to ten minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with GERD because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach. This soothing effect reduces inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, licorice root tea's natural astringent properties help to tighten the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Glycyrrhizin: Licorice tea's active compound glycyrrhizin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.

Softppepsin inhibition: Licorice root extract has been found to inhibit soft pepsin, a type of enzyme that breaks down proteins and can exacerbate acid reflux symptoms. By inhibiting this enzyme, licorice tea may help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root powder from high-quality sources following proper handling procedures.
Measure out two to three teaspoons of dried licorice root powder per eight ounce cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried licorice root powder in a large mug or teapot and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired and serve hot, allowing the flavors to mingle together for one to two minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with GERD because of its natural antacid properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and bloating. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and volatile oils, work to soothe the esophageal lining, reduce inflammation, and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and relief from discomfort. Additionally, fennel tea can help stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tea helps relax the esophageal sphincter, reducing spasms and inflammation associated with GERD, allowing food to pass smoothly through the digestive tract.

Digestive enzymes stimulant: Fennel tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach and small intestine, breaking down food more efficiently and reducing symptoms like bloating, gas, and heartburn.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with GERD, such as chest tightness and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots help soothe digestive discomforts associated with GERD.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain volatile oils that calm stomach acidity and relieve heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried fennel leaves and seeds from the foeniculum vulgare plant.
Combine one tablespoon of crushed fennel leaves and seeds with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove any solids.
Let the tea cool and then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound teas helps with GERD because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's bitter compounds also stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing symptoms like heartburn and bloating. Additionally, horehound's natural antibacterial properties help eliminate H. pylori bacteria, a common cause of stomach ulcers that can exacerbate GERD symptoms. By promoting healthy digestion and gut bacteria balance, horehound teas provide relief from persistent heartburn and acid reflux discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horehound tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from GERD symptoms like heartburn and acid reflux.

Soothing and calming effects: The tea's soothing and calming properties can help ease digestive discomfort, reducing spasms and irritation that can contribute to GERD symptoms, such as chest pain and difficulty swallowing.

Carminative properties: Horehound tea's carminative compounds can help expel gas and reduce bloating, which is often associated with GERD. This can provide relief from uncomfortable symptoms like indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used because they contain a high concentration of glycosides, which help to soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD.
Bark: The bark is used due to its rich content of alkaloids, which aid in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marrubium vulgare leaves and flowers.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with GERD because of its natural antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The tea's antibacterial agents also aid in eliminating bad bacteria that can contribute to acid reflux. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe the digestive tract, calming symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion associated with GERD, promoting a healthier digestion system overall.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Thyme tea helps neutralize stomach acid, providing fast relief from heartburn and indigestion by reducing the acidity in the stomach.
Carminative properties: Thyme's carminative properties help to ease bloating, flatulence, and discomfort by preventing gas buildup in the digestive system, which can contribute to GERD symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: Thyme tea helps relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, reducing spasms and inflammation that can cause heartburn and discomfort, allowing food to pass through the stomach more smoothly.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including heartburn and acid reflux.
Bark: The bark of the thyme plant contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a trusted source or store.
Measure one teaspoon of dried thyme leaves or two teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves per 8 oz cup water.
Heat water in a kettle or pot to boiling point then let it cool down for 5 minutes.
Steep the thyme mixture in the hot water for 7-10 minutes allowing flavors to meld and infuse.
Strain tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth then serve hot or cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram teas helps with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The herb's essential oils, such as thymol and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, marjoram's carminative properties help to reduce bloating and gas, further alleviating discomfort associated with GERD. By promoting relaxation and digestion, marjoram teas can provide relief from Gerd-related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marjoram tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of GERD.

Antioxidant properties: Marjoram tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the esophageal mucosa from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with GERD.

Soothing compounds: Marjoram tea contains soothing compounds like linalool and beta-caryophyllene, which can help relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce inflammation in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD.
Seeds: The seeds contain a compound called borneol, which has natural antacid properties that can neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh marjoram leaves in late summer or early fall for optimal flavor.
Dry the harvested marjoram leaves using a dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air-drying for 3 days.
Measure one teaspoon of dried marjoram leaves per 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to desired temperature and drink immediately or store in refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with GERD because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. The herbal tea's carnosic acid also increases gastric motility, promoting the efficient emptying of the stomach and reducing the likelihood of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus. This helps alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation, providing relief from GERD discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which is often a contributor to GERD symptoms.

Digestive enzymes stimulation: Rosemary tea contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can stimulate digestive enzyme production, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of reflux, nausea, and bloating associated with GERD.

Gastric acid reduction: The essential oils present in rosemary tea, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to decrease gastric acid secretion, which can help alleviate heartburn and discomfort caused by GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the esophageal muscles and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosemary leaves from your garden or store-bought supply measuring about one tablespoon for every eight ounces of water.
Rinse the rosemary leaves with clean filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine one tablespoon of dried rosemary leaves or two tablespoons of fresh rosemary leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the rosemary mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse into the water releasing their fragrance and flavor.
Strain the tea using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth into a cup discarding the solids and enjoy warm or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the esophageal lining. The herbs in sage tea, particularly rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort associated with GERD. Additionally, sage's carminative properties help ease digestion and prevent gas buildup, further alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Sage tea has antacid properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of heartburn and indigestion associated with GERD.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating discomfort and pain caused by GERD.
Carminative properties: Sage tea has carminative properties that can help expel gas from the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant has a calming effect on the esophageal sphincter, which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried or fresh salvia officinalis leaves for a minimum of 2 tablespoons.
Combine the harvested leaves with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the sage's essential oils and flavonoids to release.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Serve the herbal tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with GERD because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The herbal tea contains flavonoids and limonoids, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. Additionally, the tea's acidity is gentle on the stomach, allowing it to neutralize acid reflux without exacerbating symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort, large-leaved lime teas can provide relief from GERD symptoms, making it a natural remedy for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antacid and anti-inflammatory compounds: Large-leaved lime tea contains compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids that help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of large-leaved lime tea helps to protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms and reduce the risk of complications like esophagitis and Barrett's esophagus.

Carminative and spasmolytic effects: Large-leaved lime tea has been traditionally used to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and relieve spasms, which can help reduce symptoms like chest pain, nausea, and bloating associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, which help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation associated with GERD symptoms.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce esophageal mucous membrane irritation and protect against acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves in early morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the rinsed tilia platyphyllos leaves with boiling water in a large pot.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Store the freshly brewed herbal tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux. The plantain's mucilaginous texture coats the digestive tract, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid and reducing symptoms like heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, plantain tea may also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and causing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can alleviate symptoms of GERD (Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease) such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Soil binding property: Plantain's mucilage content binds to soil particles, helping to neutralize stomach acid and soothe digestive issues like GERD by forming a protective barrier in the esophagus.
Antacid property: Plantain tea has antacid properties that help neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce symptoms of GERD such as bloating, gas, and discomfort after eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm digestive issues associated with GERD.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of plantago major, ensuring at least one-third of the leaves are free from pests and diseases.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove dirt and debris, then pat them dry with paper towels.
Measure out 2-4 teaspoons of dried plantain leaves or 6-8 tablespoons of fresh leaves for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the plantain leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and serving the tea hot or chilled.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat GERD with herbal teas is by choosing teas that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce stomach acid. Chamomile, ginger, and licorice root teas are some of the most effective options. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation. Ginger tea can help reduce nausea, stomach acid, and inflammation. Licorice root tea can help increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, which can protect it from stomach acid. It is important to note that while herbal teas can help manage GERD symptoms, they should not replace any prescribed medication or a doctor's advice.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to GERD that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux and digestive issues. Herbal teas like chamomile, ginger, and licorice root are known for their soothing properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. Chamomile tea, in particular, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and GERD. Ginger tea is another popular choice, as it can help reduce nausea, bloating, and other digestive symptoms. Licorice root tea can also help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making it a good option for those with digestive issues. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for some symptoms, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or chronic conditions. Always consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
